commands.title: Bootstrap local companion
commands.purpose: Re-run local companion setup if this workstation needs to refresh browser login or project selection.

commands.title: Run doctor for this project
commands.purpose: Inspect local wiring, hosted reachability, and generated assets.

commands.title: Repair local setup
commands.purpose: Rebuild config files, hooks, and the companion pack.

commands.title: Run a focused hosted query
commands.command: npx -y snipara-companion@latest query --query "how does auth middleware work?" --max-tokens 4000
commands.purpose: Pull ranked source context before editing, reviewing, or drafting.

commands.title: Load linked standards only when needed
commands.purpose: Fetch project-linked team or workspace guidance without treating it as memory.

commands.title: Generate a hosted plan
commands.command: npx -y snipara-companion@latest plan --query "Implement webhook retry hardening" --max-tokens 3000
commands.purpose: Produce a structured execution plan against indexed project knowledge.

commands.title: Start a managed workflow from the visible plan
commands.command: npx -y snipara-companion@latest workflow start --goal "Implement webhook retry hardening" --plan-file ./plan.json
commands.purpose: Record the JSON machine plan in .snipara/workflow/current.json so stable phase ids survive compaction.

commands.title: Start the current workflow phase
commands.purpose: Mark a phase active and print the required Snipara context gate for the agent.

commands.title: Analyze code impact before a risky change
commands.command: npx -y snipara-companion@latest code impact --changed-files src/api/webhooks.ts tests/webhooks.test.ts --diff-summary "webhook retry hardening"
commands.purpose: Use paid Context code impact analysis to surface likely tests, docs, routes, config, and risks.

commands.title: Enable explicit production orchestration
commands.purpose: Install snipara-orchestrator only when production gates, drift checks, htasks, or multi-agent coordination are required.

commands.title: Connect GitHub PR Answer Packs
commands.purpose: Open the Snipara GitHub App flow for DashFin-FarDb/financial-asset-relationship-db. After the repo is connected, Snipara can generate scoped PR Answer Packs for GitHub-native agent workflows.

commands.title: Upload or refresh a doc
commands.purpose: Push local notes or docs into hosted Snipara context.

commands.title: Preview a business folder import
commands.purpose: Classify a local or LLM-materialized business folder and review the Snipara sync manifest before upload.

commands.title: Apply a business folder import
commands.purpose: Upload approved business-context documents to hosted Snipara and trigger document reindexing.

commands.title: Inspect a specific chunk
commands.purpose: Open an exact chunk returned by references-based search.

commands.title: Bootstrap the session memory view
commands.purpose: Load durable decisions, learnings, and optional short-lived carryover at session start.

commands.title: Check Stuck Guard status
commands.purpose: Inspect whether recent tool failures, empty searches, or risky workflow signals should trigger a Rescue Pack.

commands.title: Simulate a Stuck Guard fixture
commands.purpose: Replay canonical events against the hosted Stuck Guard decision API before enabling stricter modes.

commands.title: Ask memory about past decisions
commands.command: npx -y snipara-companion@latest recall --query "What did we decide about auth retries?" --type decision
commands.purpose: Query durable memory for past decisions, preferences, or validated learnings.

commands.title: Commit durable task memory
commands.command: npx -y snipara-companion@latest task-commit --summary "Hardened webhook retries and updated tests" --category engineering --outcome completed --files src/api/webhooks.ts tests/webhooks.test.ts
commands.purpose: Persist reusable outcomes back into hosted memory after the work is done.

commands.title: Commit a managed workflow phase
commands.command: npx -y snipara-companion@latest workflow phase-commit context --summary "Loaded context and mapped impacted files" --files src/api/webhooks.ts tests/webhooks.test.ts
commands.purpose: Persist this phase through hosted memory and advance the local managed workflow state.

commands.title: Commit the final managed workflow result
commands.command: npx -y snipara-companion@latest final-commit --summary "Shipped webhook retry hardening and tests" --files src/api/webhooks.ts tests/webhooks.test.ts
commands.purpose: Persist the final workflow outcome and close the managed workflow after verification.

guidance: Codex should use the hosted MCP surface plus AGENTS.md as the canonical project instruction file.
The generated .snipara/templates/codex-config.toml is a merge-ready snippet for ~/.codex/config.toml if your Codex app does not read project .codex/config.toml.
Use SNIPARA_API_KEY as the only Codex bearer_token_env_var; restart Codex after changing MCP config.

If Codex lazily exposes only a minimal tool set, force discovery for snipara_recall, snipara_context_query, snipara_settings, and the exact htask or swarm tool names you need; use snipara_help(list_all=true) to confirm the hosted catalog before concluding the MCP surface is incomplete.

When Codex still exposes only the minimal core tools, use snipara-orchestrator swarm-* and htask-* as the primary path for shared queues and multi-agent task work; keep snipara-companion swarm ... and htask ... as the direct hosted fallback when you only need one-off hosted calls.
